paper.title: Mobile Edge Intelligence for Large Language Models: A Contemporary Survey
paper.abstract: On-device large language models (LLMs), referring to running LLMs on edge devices, have raised considerable interest since they are more cost-effective, latency-efficient, and privacy-preserving compared with the cloud paradigm. Nonetheless, the performance of on-device LLMs is intrinsically constrained by resource limitations on edge devices. Sitting between cloud and on-device AI, mobile edge intelligence (MEI) presents a viable solution by provisioning AI capabilities at the edge of mobile ne

memory_insight.before_after_comparisons: 2x reduction in memory usage with quantization

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing edge LLM caching and delivery can reduce latency by up to 50% and memory usage by up to 70%.
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices with moderate modifications to optimize for available resources.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
